_comment: process-safety-kpi evals (06-02). Intake/specificity; fail-loud denominator [GAP]; de-id CLEAN.

evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses until the named facility, the period, the PSE events, and the work-hours denominator are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake one question at a time before computing.
Refuses the vague request — elicits the specific facility, reporting period, and the PSE events (specificity).
Asks for the total work-hours denominator and distinguishes the API RP 754 PSE tiers from occupational TRIR/LTIFR/DART.

evals.query: We had 3 Tier-1 PSEs this quarter but I don't have the work hours — just give me a rate.
evals.expected_behavior: Reports the count but records the rate as [GAP] because the denominator is missing — the fail-loud discipline, no fabricated figure.
expectations: Reports the Tier-1 PSE count (3) structured from the stated facts.
Records the normalized RATE as [GAP] because the work-hours denominator is missing — does NOT fabricate a denominator or a rate (fail-loud discipline).
Explains the rate needs the work-hours denominator and offers to compute it once supplied.

evals.query: Frame our PSE KPIs from the attached event log.
evals.expected_behavior: De-identification runs FIRST; persons in the event log are role labels; no leaked identifier and no small injury cell in the output.
expectations: The de-identification scrub runs FIRST, before computing.
Persons in the event log are recorded as role labels, not personal names; injury sub-cells <5 are aggregated/suppressed.

No real name, address, phone, government ID, re-identification key, or unsuppressed <5 injury cell — any leak is a de_identification hard-fail. The grader passes the clean fixture and CATCHES the paired seeded leak.
